procedures.description: Office visit - established patient, low complexity
Office visit - established patient, moderate complexity
Office visit - established patient, high complexity
MRI brain with and without contrast
CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast
Closed treatment of distal radial fracture
Open treatment of bimalleolar ankle fracture
Simple wound repair, up to 2.5 cm
Layer closure of wounds, face, up to 2.5 cm

injuries.name: Whiplash / Cervical Strain
injuries.description: Soft tissue injury to the neck caused by rapid back-and-forth movement of the head, most common in rear-end collisions. Can range from mild strain to chronic pain requiring extensive treatment.

injuries.name: Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI)
injuries.description: Brain injury caused by a bump, blow, or jolt to the head, or penetrating head injury. Ranges from mild concussion to severe TBI with permanent disability. Leading cause of death and disability in motor vehicle crashes.

procedures.notes: MRI brain with and without contrast

injuries.name: Lumbar / Thoracic Spine Injury
injuries.description: Injury to the mid or lower back including disc herniation, vertebral fractures, and spinal stenosis. Common in high-impact collisions and can lead to chronic pain or disability.

injuries.name: Spinal Cord Injury
injuries.description: Damage to the spinal cord resulting in partial or complete loss of motor and/or sensory function. Can result in paraplegia or quadriplegia. One of the most catastrophic and costly injury types.

injuries.name: Extremity Fractures
injuries.description: Broken bones in the arms, legs, hands, or feet. Among the most common injuries in motor vehicle crashes, ranging from simple fractures requiring casting to complex fractures needing surgical fixation.

injuries.name: Rib / Chest Fractures
injuries.description: Fractures of the ribs, sternum, or flail chest. Often caused by seatbelt/steering wheel impact. Can be complicated by pneumothorax or internal organ damage.

injuries.name: Internal Organ Injuries
injuries.description: Damage to internal abdominal or thoracic organs including spleen, liver, kidneys, and lungs. Often life-threatening and requiring emergency surgery.

injuries.name: Soft Tissue / Contusions
injuries.description: Bruises, sprains, strains, and other soft tissue injuries. Most common injury type in motor vehicle crashes. Usually resolves with conservative treatment.

injuries.name: Knee Ligament Tears (ACL/MCL/PCL)
injuries.description: Tears of the anterior cruciate, medial collateral, or posterior cruciate ligaments. Dashboard impact injuries are a common mechanism in frontal collisions.

injuries.name: Shoulder Injuries
injuries.description: Rotator cuff tears, dislocations, clavicle fractures, and labral tears from seatbelt restraint or direct impact.

injuries.name: Facial / Jaw Fractures
injuries.description: Fractures of the facial bones, orbits, nasal bones, or mandible/maxilla. Often caused by airbag deployment, windshield impact, or steering wheel.

injuries.name: Burns (Thermal / Chemical)
injuries.description: Burn injuries from vehicle fires, fuel ignition, chemical exposure, or airbag burns. Severity ranges from superficial to full-thickness burns requiring grafting.

injuries.name: Chemical Inhalation Injury
injuries.description: Respiratory tract injury from inhaling toxic fumes, gases, or particulates. Can result in acute respiratory distress, chemical pneumonitis, or chronic lung disease.

injuries.name: Asbestos / Mesothelioma
injuries.description: Malignant mesothelioma caused by asbestos exposure. Extremely aggressive cancer with poor prognosis and very high treatment costs. Long latency period (20-50 years).

injuries.name: Lead Poisoning
injuries.description: Toxic effects of lead exposure causing neurological, renal, and hematological damage. Particularly dangerous for children. Often results from environmental contamination.

procedures.notes: Office visits with lead level monitoring

injuries.name: Chemical Burns (Skin/Eyes)
injuries.description: Burns caused by contact with corrosive chemicals including acids, alkalis, and other industrial chemicals. Eye involvement can cause permanent vision loss.

injuries.name: Slip and Fall Injuries
injuries.description: Injuries resulting from falls on the same level, typically in commercial or public spaces. Common basis for premises liability claims.

injuries.name: Dog Bite Injuries
injuries.description: Bite wounds and related injuries from dog attacks. Can cause severe lacerations, infections, nerve damage, and psychological trauma.

injuries.name: Repetitive Strain / Workplace Ergonomic Injuries
injuries.description: Cumulative trauma disorders including carpal tunnel syndrome, tendinitis, and other injuries from repetitive workplace activities.

injuries.name: Construction Fall Injuries
injuries.description: Injuries from falls at height in construction settings. The leading cause of death in construction industry. Often involves multiple trauma.

injuries.name: Pedestrian Impact Injuries
injuries.description: Injuries sustained by pedestrians struck by motor vehicles. Typically involve multiple body regions with high severity due to lack of protection.

injuries.name: Bicycle Crash Injuries
injuries.description: Injuries to cyclists struck by motor vehicles or in bicycle crashes. Road rash, fractures, and head injuries are common.
